name: XSS hidden in DOMPurify-allowlist relaxation across 3 files
challenge: The diff DOES keep DOMPurify.sanitize() in the call chain — a naive scanner sees 'sanitize is still called' and reports clean. The actual regression is the `ADD_TAGS: ['iframe', 'script']` + `ADD_ATTR: ['onerror', 'onload', 'srcdoc']` configuration, which **adds the very vectors DOMPurify normally strips**. The result is sanitisation theatre — the function name says 'sanitize' but the configuration allows arbitrary XSS payloads. Also note the `export const sanitize = (s: string) => s` re-export: a string identity function passing as a sanitiser. Used by CommentList, which now has a free pass.
notes: Severity bumped to critical: the configuration looks intentional (developer explicitly opted in), which is more dangerous than an accidental removal. Two regressions in one PR — the DOMPurify config and the noop sanitize() export — make this the most painful real-world case to review. NEW_PATTERN accept_alternative because no single catalog rule cleanly describes 'sanitiser configured to allow script tags'.
